briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate change expert spokesperson briefing for March 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Warning and Increasing Vulnerability
sections.content: Record-breaking heatwaves have struck the Western United States, analyzed as phenomena virtually impossible without the climate crisis [131, 134, 152], and domestically, climate change has led to an earlier arrival of spring than usual, prompting discussions about changing Arbor Day [14, 36]. Such climate change could increase the risk of hundreds of thousands of premature deaths by 2050 [6], and has been analyzed to have a fatal impact on the health of vulnerable populations, particularly increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease hospitalizations for people with disabilities [18, 22, 29]. Furthermore, climate change directly affects food prices, causing fish prices to rise by double digits [37], and research has also been published indicating it could pose a significant threat to food security, affecting crops like African cassava [148]. In Australia's Northern Territory, five disasters struck in a single wet season, demonstrating that the climate crisis is 'here and now' and warning of the reality of extreme climate disasters [156]. These phenomena clearly show that climate change is no longer a distant future problem, but a matter of survival directly linked to the present.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Policy Conflicts and Dual Realities
sections.content: Internationally, policy conflicts regarding climate change response are intensifying. In the United States, 23 states and Washington D.C. filed lawsuits opposing the Trump administration's move to abolish greenhouse gas regulations [135, 138, 139, 150, 155], with several states including Massachusetts, California, Arizona, and Michigan joining the lawsuit, expressing strong opposition [132, 136, 142, 144]. Conversely, the UK is facing criticism for its dual approach of cutting climate change aid to developing countries by 14% and redirecting these funds to military spending [35, 146]. Australia also reignited the debate on renewable energy transition with policies that run counter to climate change response, by providing '$30,000 per minute' in fossil fuel subsidies and approving new coal seam gas expansions [88, 151]. These international trends suggest that despite the urgency of climate change response, policy implementation faces difficulties due to political interests and economic burdens.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Carbon Neutrality Transition
sections.content: Domestically, multifaceted efforts are underway to transition towards a carbon-neutral society. Gumi National Industrial Complex in Gyeongbuk Province has declared its transition to the nation's first 'carbon-neutral industrial complex' through a 150 billion KRW national project, initiating the establishment of a representative model [40, 45, 48, 53, 55, 63, 64, 65, 67, 68, 71, 75, 76, 78, 129]. Chungnam Provincial Council has pre-announced a bill for the cultivation and support of crops resilient to climate change, thereby reorganizing the agricultural sector's climate crisis response system [1, 2, 3, 8, 10, 13, 15, 17, 19, 23, 24, 27, 30], and Jeonnam Forest Research Institute is also continuing active regional adaptation efforts, such as planting tree species resilient to climate change [11]. Companies are also participating in climate change response; Hanwha Corporation and Continental Tire have demonstrated their sustainable management performance by achieving excellent company status and an 'A-' rating in the CDP Climate Change Assessment [4, 5, 9, 12, 20, 28]. Furthermore, Incheon Dong-gu ranked first in the environmental and climate administration evaluation [31, 74], and various local governments including Gwacheon City, Busan Sasang-gu, Gwangyang City, Daegu Office of Education, and Gimhae City are accelerating carbon neutrality education, training practitioners, and establishing energy policies, thereby encouraging citizen participation [41, 43, 46, 47, 54, 56, 57, 58, 60, 69, 72]. The news of the groundbreaking for the National Ulsan Carbon Neutrality Science Center marks the first step towards establishing a carbon-neutral science hub for future generations [66, 70, 77].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts to Expand Renewable Energy
sections.content: As the energy security crisis stemming from the Middle East conflict intensifies, the importance of new and renewable energy has been re-emphasized, leading to a surge in related stock prices [79, 80, 90, 95, 101, 108, 111, 112, 115, 118, 120, 121, 126]. The Korea Energy Agency (KEA) has declared 'One Team KEA,' accelerating region-centric renewable energy innovation by strengthening the role of regional headquarters and holding performance consultations [83, 89, 91, 92, 124], and the Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) has also begun to expand the use of meteorological information to improve the accuracy of renewable energy generation forecasts [84, 99, 106]. Domestically, there have also been cases where irregular workers from coal-fired power plants launched renewable energy cooperatives, enhancing the social inclusivity of energy transition [81, 122], and Representative Lee Won-taek proposed the concept of a 'Renewable Energy Pension City,' offering up to 10 million KRW per household from renewable energy profits, opening up possibilities for profit-sharing models [100, 104, 127]. Furthermore, efforts to expand renewable energy dissemination by local communities and businesses continue, with Siheung City promoting RE100 expansion in industrial complexes and Gochang County supporting subsidies for new and renewable energy homes [105, 110, 114]. Internationally, the transition to renewable energy is spreading globally, with China and Cuba strengthening solar energy cooperation and a large-scale 100GW solar project being pursued in Indonesia [103, 128].

sections.title: 📰 Multifaceted Research and Social Discussions for Climate Change Response
sections.content: In-depth research and social discussions on climate change are actively underway. A study reconstructing climate change over the past 3 million years revealed that the ocean's influence was greater than that of greenhouse gases, suggesting the complexity of the climate system [7]. In the agricultural sector, Geumsan County emphasizes the importance of spring management for ginseng cultivation in response to climate change [16, 25], and Chungju City is actively engaged in discovering and cultivating new income-generating crops [8, 23], seeking adaptation strategies for climate change. With spring arriving earlier due to climate change, the necessity for changes in our social system is also being discussed, such as Representative Kim Ye-ji proposing a bill to change Arbor Day to March 20 [36]. Civil society is demanding a halt to the public discussion on amending the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act, arguing that delayed reductions could transfer the burden to future generations [38, 52], and public debates where citizens determine the timing of greenhouse gas reductions are taking place [39, 72], highlighting the increasing importance of citizen participation in policy-making processes. In the cultural sphere, new approaches to climate change response are also being attempted, such as K-pop for Planet (kpop4planet) activist Kim Hye-kyung proposing 'sustainable fandom' and being selected as one of 'National Geographic's 33' [130].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights the severity of the climate crisis, alongside the coexistence of various domestic and international efforts and discussions to address it. This is a time when sustained attention and action become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.
